tools.name: list_services
tools.description: The service categories vendors on Mediawork offer, such as Editorial, Sound Post or Visual Effects. Each has a uuid — pass it as `service` to search_facilities to filter the directory to vendors offering that service. Call this first when a question is about a kind of work.

tools.name: list_places
tools.description: Cities, states and countries that have at least one vendor listed, with a count of how many. Use a place `slug` as the `place` argument to search_facilities.

tools.name: search_facilities
tools.description: Search publicly listed post-production facilities, distribution vendors and creative companies. Filter by free text, by place slug (from list_places) and by service uuid (from list_services). To find vendors somewhere, prefer `place`; a `query` naming a place is treated as one anyway, and the respon

tools.name: get_facility
tools.description: The full public profile for one vendor: services offered, creatives on the team and what each of them does, production types, rooms and spaces, and addresses. Takes the `companySlug` and `facilitySlug` pair returned by search_facilities.

tools.name: search_faq
tools.description: Mediawork's published FAQ, grouped by category. With a query, returns matching questions and their answers; without one, returns every question so you can pick which to search for.

tools.name: list_blog_posts
tools.description: Published posts from the Mediawork blog, newest first. Use get_blog_post with a returned `slug` to read one in full.

tools.name: get_blog_post
tools.description: The full body of one published blog post, as markdown.

tools.name: get_plans
tools.description: Mediawork's subscription plans: what each includes, and its prices. Prices are published in several currencies — pass `currency` to return just one.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search everything Mediawork publishes — vendor directory listings, FAQ entries and blog posts — and return matching records as {id, title, url}. Pass an `id` to `fetch` to read one in full. If your client supports them, the dedicated tools (search_facilities, search_faq, …) give richer, filterable r

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Retrieve the full contents of one record by the `id` returned from `search`.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_services, search_facilities, list_blog_posts, get_blog_post, search · Private data: search, fetch · Public sink: list_services, search_facilities, get_blog_post.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_services, search_facilities, list_blog_posts, get_blog_post, search · Private data: search, fetch · Public sink: list_services, search_facilities, get_blog_post

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
